questions.held_out_passage: When the spirit swells my breast I love to roam leisurely among the green hills; or sometimes, sitting on the brink of the murmuring Missouri, I marvel at the great blue overhead.

questions.text: How does Zitkala-Sa interpret the incident of the burning taper and the holy book?
questions.held_out_passage: Knocking out the chinking of our log cabin, some evil hand thrust in a burning taper of braided dry grass, but failed of his intent, for the fire died out and the half-burned brand fell inward to the floor. Directly above it, on a shelf, lay the holy book. This is what we found after our return from a several days' visit. Surely some great power is hid in the sacred book!

questions.text: What is Zitkala-Sa's reaction to the preacher's disappointment about her church attendance?
questions.held_out_passage: Every holy day (Sunday) I look about our little God's house, and not seeing you there, I am disappointed. This is why I come today.
